episodes.title: Pilot
episodes.description: Rue returns home from rehab and meets Jules; Nate and Maddy attempt to make each other jealous; Kat is pressured to lose something.

episodes.title: Stuntin' Like My Daddy
episodes.description: On the first day of school, Rue struggles to put the past behind her; Nate's obsessions turn violent; Kat finds a video of her online.

episodes.title: Made You Look
episodes.description: Kat starts camming; Jules falls for a boy online; Rue is confronted about her lies at NA; Cassie and McKay spend the weekend at his college.

episodes.title: Shook Ones Pt II
episodes.description: Rue, trying to get clean for Jules, chaperones Gia at the carnival; Kat misreads a situation; Jules encounters Cal; Nate reveals who he really is.

episodes.title: '03 Bonnie and Clyde
episodes.description: In the aftermath of the carnival, Maddy and Nate are forced to deal with a police investigation; Jules begins to feel pressured by her role in Rue's sobriety; Cal worries about his actions.

episodes.title: The Next Episode
episodes.description: On Halloween, Jules starts exhibiting concerning behavior; McKay questions his future in football; Cassie spends some time with Daniel; Kat continues to push Ethan away; Nate comes up with a plan.

episodes.title: The Trials and Tribulations of Trying to Pee While Depressed
episodes.description: Rue falls into a depressive episode and binge-watches 22 episodes of a British reality show; Jules visits an old friend; Cassie looks for advice to help her deal with the fallout of Halloween night.

episodes.title: And Salt the Earth Behind You
episodes.description: It's the winter formal at East Highland.

episodes.title: Trying to Get to Heaven Before They Close the Door
episodes.description: Rue and Jules cross paths for the first time since Christmas as East Highland rings in the new year.

episodes.title: Out of Touch
episodes.description: As the semester kicks off, Jules questions Rue and Elliot's new friendship. The lines between fantasy and reality begin to blur as Kat considers her relationship.

episodes.title: Ruminations: Big and Little Bullys
episodes.description: Maddy considers getting back together with Nate, Rue pursues a new business venture as she helps to form a friendship between Jules and Elliot.

episodes.title: You Who Cannot See, Think of Those Who Can
episodes.description: At Maddy's birthday party, relationships are celebrated and questioned. Jules turns to Elliot for advice. Cal takes a trip down memory lane.

episodes.title: Stand Still Like the Hummingbird
episodes.description: Fate has a way of catching up with those who try to outrun it.

episodes.title: A Thousand Little Trees of Blood
episodes.description: As relationships with mothers are tested, Rue tries to recover. Nate enjoys his newfound freedom.

episodes.title: The Theater and Its Double
episodes.description: Art imitates life as the East Highland crew watches their complicated dynamics reflected in Lexi's long-awaited play.

episodes.title: All My Life, My Heart Has Yearned for a Thing I Cannot Name
episodes.description: Snippets of memory collide with the present and future.

episodes.title: The Cage
episodes.description: Capt. Pike is held prisoner and tested by aliens who have the power to project incredibly lifelike illusions.

episodes.title: The Man Trap
episodes.description: Dr. McCoy discovers his old flame is not what she seems after crew members begin dying from a sudden lack of salt in their bodies.

episodes.title: Charlie X
episodes.description: Captain Kirk must learn the limits to the power of a 17-year-old boy with the psionic ability to create anything and destroy anyone.

episodes.title: Where No Man Has Gone Before
episodes.description: The flight recorder of the 200-year-old U.S.S. Valiant relays a tale of terror--a magnetic storm at the edge of the galaxy.

episodes.title: The Naked Time
episodes.description: The crew is infected with a mysterious disease that removes people's emotional inhibitions to a dangerous degree.

episodes.title: The Enemy Within
episodes.description: A transporter malfunction splits Captain Kirk into two halves: one meek and indecisive, the other violent and ill tempered. The remaining crew members stranded on the planet cannot be beamed up to the ship until a problem is fixed.

episodes.title: Mudd's Women
episodes.description: The Enterprise picks up untrustworthy entrepreneur Harry Mudd accompanied by three beautiful women who immediately put a spell on all the male crew members.

episodes.title: What Are Little Girls Made Of?
episodes.description: Nurse Chapel is reunited with her fiancé; but his new obsession leads him to make an android duplicate of Captain Kirk.

episodes.title: Miri
episodes.description: The Enterprise discovers a planet exactly like Earth, but the only inhabitants are children who contract a fatal disease upon entering puberty.

episodes.title: Dagger of the Mind
episodes.description: Kirk and psychiatrist Helen Noel are trapped on a maximum security penal colony that experiments with mind control and Spock must use the Vulcan mind-meld to find a way to save them.

episodes.title: The Corbomite Maneuver
episodes.description: After the Enterprise is forced to destroy a dangerous marker buoy, a gigantic alien ship arrives to capture and condemn the crew as trespassers.

episodes.title: The Menagerie: Part I
episodes.description: Spock kidnaps the disabled Capt. Pike, hijacks the Enterprise, and then surrenders for court martial.

episodes.title: The Menagerie: Part II
episodes.description: At Spock's court martial, he explains himself with mysterious footage about when Capt. Pike was kidnapped by powerful illusion casting aliens.

episodes.title: The Conscience of the King
episodes.description: While Captain Kirk investigates whether an actor is actually a presumed dead mass murderer, a mysterious assailant is killing the people who could identify the fugitive.

episodes.title: Balance of Terror
episodes.description: The Enterprise must decide on its response when a Romulan ship makes a destructively hostile armed probe of Federation territory.

episodes.title: Shore Leave
episodes.description: The past months have left the crew exhausted and in desperate need of a break, but does this explain McCoy's encounter with a human-sized white rabbit or Kirk crossing paths with the prankster who plagued his days at Starfleet Academy?

episodes.title: The Galileo Seven
episodes.description: The Galileo, under Spock's command, crash-lands on a hostile planet. As the Enterprise races against time to find the shuttlecraft, Spock's strictly logical leadership clashes with the fear and resentment of his crew.

episodes.title: The Squire of Gothos
episodes.description: A being that controls matter and creates planets wants to play with the Enterprise crew.

episodes.title: Arena
episodes.description: For bringing hostility into their solar system, a superior alien race brings Captain Kirk into mortal combat against the reptilian captain of an alien ship he was pursuing.

episodes.title: Tomorrow Is Yesterday
episodes.description: The Enterprise is thrown back in time to 1960s Earth.

episodes.title: Court Martial
episodes.description: Kirk draws a court martial in the negligent death of a crewman.

episodes.title: The Return of the Archons
episodes.description: Seeking the answer to a century-old mystery, Kirk and crew encounter a vacantly peaceful society under a 6000-year autocratic rule that kills all those it can't absorb.

episodes.title: Space Seed
episodes.description: While on patrol in deep space, Captain Kirk and his crew find and revive a genetically-engineered world conqueror and his compatriots from Earth's Twentieth Century.

episodes.title: A Taste of Armageddon
episodes.description: Kirk and Spock must save their ship's crew when they are all declared killed in action in a bizarre computer simulated war where the actual deaths must nevertheless occur.

episodes.title: This Side of Paradise
episodes.description: The Enterprise investigates a planet whose colonists should be dead, but are not.

episodes.title: The Devil in the Dark
episodes.description: The Enterprise is sent to a mining colony that is being terrorized by a mysterious monster, only to find that the situation is not that simple.

episodes.title: Errand of Mercy
episodes.description: With a war with Klingons raging, Kirk and Spock attempt to resist an occupation of a planet with incomprehensibly placid natives.

episodes.title: The Alternative Factor
episodes.description: Existence itself comes under threat from a man's power-struggle with his alternate self, with the Enterprise's strained dilithium crystals presenting his key to a final solution.

episodes.title: The City on the Edge of Forever
episodes.description: When a temporarily insane Dr. McCoy accidentally changes history and destroys his time, Kirk and Spock follow him to prevent the disaster, but the price to do so is high.

episodes.title: Operation -- Annihilate!
episodes.description: The Enterprise crew attempts to stop a plague of amoeba-like creatures from possessing human hosts and spreading throughout the galaxy.

episodes.title: Amok Time
episodes.description: In the throes of his Pon Farr mating period, Spock must return to Vulcan to meet his intended future wife, betrothed from childhood.

episodes.title: The Child
episodes.description: Deanna is impregnated by an unknown alien life-form, and Dr. Katherine Pulaski joins the Enterprise as the ship's new Chief Medical Officer.

episodes.title: Where Silence Has Lease
episodes.description: The Enterprise encounters a mysterious void in space, and when they move in closer to investigate further, it envelops them and they can't get out.

episodes.title: Elementary, Dear Data
episodes.description: An attempt to provide Data with a challenging Sherlock Holmes holodeck program backfires when its Professor Moriarty character accidentally becomes self-aware.

episodes.title: The Outrageous Okona
episodes.description: The Enterprise rescues the captain of a broken-down freighter, only to become involved in a dispute between feuding worlds, each demanding custody of their guest. Data seeks help from Guinan in understanding humor.

episodes.title: Loud as a Whisper
episodes.description: The crew ferries a deaf mediator to Solais V to negotiate an end to a civil war.

episodes.title: The Schizoid Man
episodes.description: A dying anti-social scientist unintentionally comes upon the perfect vessel in which to preserve his intellect and arrogant personality - Data.

episodes.title: Unnatural Selection
episodes.description: Enhanced DNA developments trigger an epidemic of rapid aging. Caught among its victims - Dr. Pulaski.

episodes.title: A Matter of Honor
episodes.description: Under an Officers Exchange Program, Riker serves aboard a Klingon ship, whose aging captain seeks an unwarranted bloody retaliation for suspected Enterprise treachery.

episodes.title: The Measure of a Man
episodes.description: When Data resigns his commission rather than be dismantled for examination by an inadequately skilled scientist, a formal hearing is convened to determine whether Data is considered property without rights or is a sentient being.

episodes.title: The Dauphin
episodes.description: Wesley falls for the young future leader of Daled IV, unaware just how dangerous the girl's protective and overly restrictive guardian can be.

episodes.title: Contagion
episodes.description: Encountering the superior technology of a very ancient race creates system malfunctions for a doomed Federation starship, for the Enterprise, and for an eavesdropping Romulan warbird.

episodes.title: The Royale
episodes.description: Worf, Data, and Riker find themselves trapped in a re-creation of a poorly written novel.

episodes.title: Time Squared
episodes.description: The Enterprise comes across its own shuttlecraft adrift in space - a duplicate - with an unconscious duplicate Capt. Picard inside.

episodes.title: The Icarus Factor
episodes.description: Riker's delight at being offered a command of the USS Ares turns to frustration when the man sent to prepare him for his mission is his estranged father; Worf's behavior leads Wesley to delve into Klingon tradition.

episodes.title: Pen Pals
episodes.description: Data befriends an alien girl in distress, breaking the Prime Directive, while Wesley commands his first team.

episodes.title: Q Who
episodes.description: Q tries to prove that Picard needs him as part of their crew by hurling the Enterprise 7,000 light years away where they encounter the Borg for the first time.

episodes.title: Samaritan Snare
episodes.description: With Picard away for routine surgery, the Enterprise responds to an apparent distress call from a Pakled vessel.

episodes.title: Up the Long Ladder
episodes.description: An antiquated distress signal leads to two lost 22nd century Earth colonies, each facing doom in different ways, one by fire, the other by prolonged cloning.

episodes.title: Manhunt
episodes.description: A midlife Betazoid condition renders Ambassador Troi hot on the hunt for romance and wedlock with Captain Picard - or any man.

episodes.title: The Emissary
episodes.description: The Enterprise addresses the emergency of an old Klingon ship coming out of stasis and ready to fight the Federation. A half-Human/half Klingon emissary arrives to help, who once knew Worf intimately.

episodes.title: Peak Performance
episodes.description: The crew of the Enterprise participates in war games.

episodes.title: Shades of Gray
episodes.description: When Commander Riker comes down with a dangerous alien infection after an away mission, the only way to treat it may be through reliving his memories.

episodes.title: Pilot
episodes.description: The senior members of the White House staff are summoned to the office early in the morning to handle difficult situations: Hundreds of Cubans are on their way across the Atlantic Ocean in search of freedom.

episodes.title: Post Hoc, Ergo Propter Hoc
episodes.description: Leo hires Mandy as a consultant to the President. Vice President Hoynes rebuffs C.J. when she tries to discuss a comment he made. Leo confronts Hoynes and insists that he understand his obligation to communicate with the press secretary.

episodes.title: A Proportional Response
episodes.description: When Danny, a reporter, learns about Sam's relationship with a call girl, he doesn't write a story about it because C.J. gives Danny a lead on a much bigger newsworthy item in exchange for his silence.

episodes.title: Five Votes Down
episodes.description: The staff has 72 hours to rescue a gun-control bill that needs five more votes in Congress to pass. Eventually they get four votes, so Leo reluctantly visits the vice president, and Hoynes promises he'll get the other vote.

episodes.title: The Crackpots and These Women
episodes.description: Leo assigns his senior staff the task of conducting private meetings with fringe groups that want attention from the White House. Toby discovers he was not the President's first choice for communications director.

episodes.title: Mr. Willis of Ohio
episodes.description: A mentally unbalanced woman with a gun climbs the White House fence. The Secret Service foils her plan to target Zoey. Toby and Mandy convince the husband of a deceased congresswoman to vote against a census amendment in committee.

episodes.title: The State Dinner
episodes.description: The President and First Lady host a tense dinner at the White House honoring the president of Indonesia and his wife. Danny flirts with C.J. The President takes action against a group of survivalists in Idaho.

episodes.title: Enemies
episodes.description: The President embarrasses Vice President Hoynes during a cabinet meeting, and Danny finds out. In order to keep the story from print, C.J. arranges a meeting between Danny and the President.

episodes.title: The Short List
episodes.description: The President confidentially chooses Judge Harrison as his nominee for the Supreme Court. His staff continues to search Harrison's past.

episodes.title: In Excelsis Deo
episodes.description: When a homeless veteran dies on the National Mall and his body remains uncollected for hours, Toby becomes fixated on getting him a proper burial.

episodes.title: Lord John Marbury
episodes.description: When India sends troops into Kashmir, President Bartlett calls for a British former ambassador to India to help out - over Leo's strong objections.

episodes.title: He Shall, from Time to Time...
episodes.description: The President and his staff prepare the State of the Union Address, but the President becomes ill. His naval doctor examines him in the living quarters. Mrs. Bartlet, a physician, orders various medical tests.

episodes.title: Take Out the Trash Day
episodes.description: The parents of a brutally murdered gay student are invited to witness the President sign a hate crimes bill into law even though the father doesn't believe that Bartlet expresses a strong enough position on gay rights.

episodes.title: Take This Sabbath Day
episodes.description: On a Friday evening, the United States Supreme Court rejects the application for a stay of execution for Simon Cruz. The President of the United States is the only person capable of commuting a federal sentence.

episodes.title: Celestial Navigation
episodes.description: Josh lectures to a large group of college students, recounting one day in the life of the harried senior White House staff. Meanwhile, Sam and Toby rush to Connecticut to help Supreme Court Justice nominee Roberto Mendoza.

episodes.title: 20 Hours in L.A.
episodes.description: The President meets Zoey's new bodyguard on a 20-hour trip to California; Josh tries to keep a fund raiser from being canceled; Leo needs the VP to break a 50-50 tie in the Senate.

episodes.title: The White House Pro-Am
episodes.description: The First Lady's public comments on a White House appointment create problems for the President because he's afraid of incurring his wife's wrath. So Sam explains to Abby the importance of working with the President'a staff in the future.

episodes.title: Six Meetings Before Lunch
episodes.description: The morning after Mendoza's confirmation, various staff members are brought back to earth by difficult meetings.

episodes.title: Let Bartlet Be Bartlet
episodes.description: The West Wing staff are feeling malaise as it seems they never get anything accomplished. Meanwhile, a leaked memo is a land mine that could embarrass the administration.

episodes.title: Mandatory Minimums
episodes.description: A newly energized administration prepares to take on soft money, increase addiction treatment, and remove mandatory minimum sentencing.

episodes.title: Lies, Damn Lies and Statistics
episodes.description: The staff waits for a poll to tell them if their new, activist policies are moving them in the right direction. C.J. feels like her opinions are being discounted.

episodes.title: What Kind of Day Has It Been
episodes.description: An American Air Force fighter plane gets shot down over Iraq, and, eventually, the pilot is successfully rescued. The space shuttle--with Toby's astronaut brother on board--can't land because of mechanical problems.

episodes.title: In the Shadow of Two Gunmen: Part I
episodes.description: The President is rushed to hospital following a shooting; staffers field questions on protection measures and executive authority. Flashbacks show how key staff members joined the Bartlet campaign.

episodes.title: In the Shadow of Two Gunmen: Part II
episodes.description: A suspect in the presidential shooting is caught; staffers anxiously await news on Bartlet and Josh's injuries; flashbacks show how C.J. and Donna joined Bartlet's campaign.

episodes.title: The Midterms
episodes.description: In the months leading up to the elections, the staff makes an all-out effort to pick up Democratic seats in Congress. Sam betrays a friend after convincing him to run for office with a promise of support from the Democratic party.

episodes.title: In This White House
episodes.description: After a Republican commentator trounces Sam on television, President Bartlett decides to hire her over the objections of the staff. Josh and Toby mediate a conference between U.S. drug companies and an African President whose country is dying of AIDS.

episodes.title: And It's Surely to Their Credit
episodes.description: Sam helps Josh battle his health insurance company; Ainsley Hayes encounters her firebrand boss and hostile colleagues; Abby gives the President some good news about his health; C.J. discovers a retiring General intends to publicly criticize the president.

episodes.title: The Lame Duck Congress
episodes.description: The administration considers recalling Congress to pass a nuclear test ban treaty; a Ukranian politician arrives unannounced at the White House; Sam reluctantly asks Ainsley to summarize a position paper; C.J.'s personal and professional relationship with Danny becomes more complex.

episodes.title: The Portland Trip
episodes.description: C.J. joins the President on a flight to Portland after a wisecrack about Notre Dame; Josh seeks to defeat an anti-gay marriage bill; Sam wants to rewrite an education speech; an oil tanker appears to be violating UN sanctions; Josh teases Donna over her dating record.

episodes.title: Shibboleth
episodes.description: Dozens of Chinese stowaways are discovered in a container ship in California; Toby looks to pick a fight over school prayer with a recess appointment; Thanksgiving at the White House sees C.J. in charge of turkeys and Charlie looking for the ultimate carving knife.

episodes.title: Galileo
episodes.description: The President and NASA plan a TV event for a probe's landing on Mars; satellite photographs show a suspicious-looking fire in Russia; Leo asks Toby and Josh to decide on the next postage stamp; Sam and C.J. have personal reasons for not wanting to accompany the President to a concert.

episodes.title: Noël
episodes.description: Josh speaks to a psychiatrist about the events of the last three weeks: Toby hired musicians for the foyer, an Air Force pilot disobeyed orders, Yo-Yo Ma performed at the White House, and Josh managed to cut his hand quite badly.

episodes.title: The Leadership Breakfast
episodes.description: Toby wants to use a bipartisan breakfast to discuss real issues instead of making it a staged event; Sam floats the idea of moving the press room across the street; Leo wants Josh to apologize to a columnist on his behalf; Leo and Toby realize they need to start thinking about reelection.

episodes.title: The Drop In
episodes.description: Leo fails to convince Bartlet of the merits of an expensive but unproven missile defense system. Meanwhile, Leo and the others are irritated by the news that pompous Lord John Marbury has been appointed ambassador from Great Britain.

episodes.title: Bartlet's Third State of the Union
episodes.description: Bartlet's speech is a success. Unfortunately, problems immediately occur that diminish the White House staff's enthusiasm. Sam learns that a policeman whom Bartlet praised during the speech was charged with beating a suspect 17 years ago.

episodes.title: The War at Home
episodes.description: President Bartlet is fighting a war on two fronts as he tries to rescue hostages in Colombia and deal with explaining to his wife why he's breaking his word to her by running for a 2nd term.

episodes.title: Ellie
episodes.description: Comments made by Surgeon General Dr. Millicent Griffith concerning the medical effects of marijuana appear to reverse the President's stance on legalizing the drug.

episodes.title: Somebody's Going to Emergency, Somebody's Going to Jail
episodes.description: Sam finds out that his father was unfaithful to Sam's mother for 28 years. Sam tries to help a friend of Donna's whose dying father wants to see his own deceased father's reputation cleared of committing espionage during WWII.

episodes.title: The Stackhouse Filibuster
episodes.description: A Senator delays a vote for over nine hours. He reads recipes, novels and poker rulebooks to the empty chamber because he wants more stringent requirements on diagnosing and treating autism added to a child health-care reform bill.

episodes.title: 17 People
episodes.description: For days, Toby ponders the mystery of Hoynes' attempt to gain the voters' favor by admonishing the oil companies. He knows about a poll conducted for Hoynes and an upcoming visit for the vice president to Bartlet's home state.

episodes.title: Bad Moon Rising
episodes.description: President Bartlett talks to the White House Counsel about the concealment of his MS. An enraged Toby has CJ grill the West Wing staff to find a leaker. Josh and Donna spar over a financial bailout of Mexico.

episodes.title: The Fall's Gonna Kill You
episodes.description: White House Counsel Oliver Babbish interviews CJ once she learns of the President's condition. The First Lady learns she may be legally vulnerable. Donna frets over news of a falling satellite.

episodes.title: 18th and Potomac
episodes.description: After Joey gives Bartlet and his staff devastating polling figures, they debate how to inform the public of Bartlet's multiple sclerosis. A major factor is the unanswered question of Bartlet's intent to seek reelection.

episodes.title: Two Cathedrals
episodes.description: Josh implements a rash plan to coerce wayward Democrats to support the administration's lawsuit against big tobacco companies. The crisis in Haiti escalates. Bartlet must decide whether to run for reelection--most Democrats hope he won't.
